prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a conductivity cell: a glass vessel holding two parallel rectangular 'platinised Pt' electrode plates a fixed distance apart, each connected by a wire emerging from the top, immersed in a pale electrolyte solution. Label: platinised Pt electrodes, fixed separation l, plate area A, 'cell constant = l/A'. Orange accent labels and thin leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: A conductivity cell — a glass tube holding two parallel platinized platinum electrode plates connected by wires, immersed in electrolyte solution

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a Wheatstone bridge as a diamond of four arms. One arm is a 'conductivity cell (R_unknown)'; the other three are resistors labelled R1, R2, R3 (one marked 'variable'). An 'AC source' connects across one diagonal and a 'detector (null point)' across the other. Label each arm and the source/detector. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical schematic style. No bright background.
alt: Wheatstone bridge diagram with the conductivity cell as the unknown resistance in one arm, three known resistances in the other arms, an AC source and a null detector

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A power source (battery) on the left drives glowing current through wires into a cell on the right where two electrodes sit in liquid, each fizzing with rising gas bubbles as a compound is forced apart. The mood is 'electricity prising a molecule open' — the reverse of a battery. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber current and cyan bubbles on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A battery driving current through a cell, forcing a compound to split apart into bubbling gases at two electrodes

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a). A vertical dark slab on the left labelled 'electrode surface'. Immediately beside it, a thin vertical orange band labelled 'interfacial region — migration'. To the right, a wide blue region labelled 'bulk solution'; within it a single positive ion (⊕) traces a jagged zig-zag random-walk path labelled 'diffusion'. An arrow near the electrode shows the field-driven 'migration' direction. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Diagram: a dark electrode surface on the left; a thin orange interfacial band labelled migration beside it; the wide blue bulk region on the right showing a positive ion taking a jagged random-walk path labelled diffusion

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A high-temperature electrolysis cell filled with glowing orange molten salt; two electrodes plunge in; at one, droplets of bright molten metal collect, at the other, pale gas bubbles rise. Industrial, fiery atmosphere — winning a reactive metal from its salt by sheer electrical force. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, intense molten-orange glow against a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A glowing cell of molten salt with electrodes, bright molten metal collecting at one electrode and gas bubbling at the other

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a molten-salt electrolysis cell glowing orange. A battery drives two electrodes. At the cathode (−), 'Na⁺ + e⁻ → Na' with molten sodium collecting; at the anode (+), 'Cl⁻ → ½Cl₂ + e⁻' with greenish gas bubbles rising. Label: molten NaCl, cathode (Na), anode (Cl₂), battery. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Molten NaCl electrolysis cell: Na+ ions moving to cathode forming molten Na, Cl- ions moving to anode forming Cl2 gas bubbles, battery driving it

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Close-up of an electrode surface where gas bubbles are forming with visible difficulty; above, a voltage gauge shows a needle pushed past the 'expected' mark into an 'extra' zone glowing amber, symbolising the overvoltage needed to make a gas. The mood is 'theory says one thing, the real electrode demands more'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cyan bubbles and amber gauge on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No readable text.
alt: Gas bubbles struggling to form on an electrode with a gauge showing an extra voltage push needed beyond the expected value

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A sweeping industrial montage from left to right: a chlor-alkali cell fizzing with gas, then rows of large copper-refining tanks with hanging metal sheets in blue electrolyte, then a freshly chrome-plated object lifted gleaming from a plating bath. The mood is 'electrolysis at industrial scale'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cool blues and warm copper tones on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A montage of industrial electrolysis: a chlor-alkali plant, rows of copper refining tanks, and a chrome-plated object emerging bright from a bath

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a brine electrolysis cell. A power source drives two electrodes in 'NaCl(aq) brine'. Cathode (−): bubbles labelled 'H₂', with '2H₂O + 2e⁻ → H₂ + 2OH⁻'. Anode (+): bubbles labelled 'Cl₂', with '2Cl⁻ → Cl₂ + 2e⁻'. Annotate the solution 'NaOH builds up (Na⁺ + OH⁻)'. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Brine electrolysis cell: cathode releasing H2 with OH- accumulating, anode releasing Cl2, Na+ spectator ions, NaOH solution

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a copper electrorefining cell in 'CuSO₄(aq)'. Left electrode labelled 'crude Cu anode (+)' shown dissolving and pitted; right electrode labelled 'pure Cu cathode (−)' shown smooth and growing. Arrows: 'Cu²⁺' migrating from anode to cathode; small ions 'Zn²⁺, Fe²⁺' staying dissolved; a heap of yellow 'anode mud (Ag, Au, Pt)' beneath the anode. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Electrorefining cell: impure Cu anode (+) dissolving, pure Cu cathode (-) growing, CuSO4 electrolyte, Zn2+/Fe2+ staying in solution, gold/silver anode mud at the bottom
